hero.title: Better retros,
hero.subtitle: A private, real-time retrospective board for Scrum and Kanban teams. Reflect honestly, vote together, and ship action items that stick.
hero.freeNotice: Free to use · No credit card required · Works with any team

features.title: Everything your team needs for a great retrospective.
features.subtitle: Built for teams who care about continuous improvement — not just ticking a ceremony box.
features.privateRoomsDesc: Password-protected spaces where your team can speak freely. No uninvited observers, ever.
features.realtimeDesc: Cards, votes, and status updates appear instantly for every participant — no refreshing required.
features.anonymousDesc: Let your team share honest feedback without their names attached. Psychological safety, built in.
features.actionItemsDesc: Capture outcomes directly on the board and track them to completion — no external tool needed.

steps.title: Up and running in minutes.
steps.subtitle: No onboarding calls. No complex setup. Just open a room and start reflecting.
steps.step1Desc: Name your retro, choose your columns, and protect it with a password. Ready in under a minute.
steps.step2Desc: Share the room link and password with your teammates. Anyone with the link can join.
steps.step3Desc: Add cards, vote on what matters most, discuss as a team, and close with clear action items.

why.title: Built different, on purpose.
why.subtitle: Four things that set RetroDash apart from a generic retro tool.
why.gamificationDesc: Every retro closes with a ranking. Gold, silver, and bronze for top contributors — a light way to celebrate participation.
why.carryOverDesc: Unfinished actions from past sprints land automatically in your next room. Continuity between sessions, zero manual work.
why.anonymousModeDesc: When active, no names or photos appear anywhere — not even on the scoreboard. Honest feedback without social pressure.
why.themingDesc: The interface respects your OS setting or lets you pick manually. Switches instantly, no page reload needed.

useCasesTeaser.title: Works for every kind of team.
useCasesTeaser.subtitle: From weekly sprint retros to async post-mortems, RetroDash adapts to your process.
useCasesTeaser.scrumDesc: Structured sprint retrospectives with custom columns, dot voting, and action items that carry forward every sprint.
useCasesTeaser.remoteDesc: Participants join at their own pace. Anonymous mode removes social pressure so honest feedback flows freely.
useCasesTeaser.productDesc: Tie action items directly to feedback cards. Track outcomes across sprints with the carry-over system.

cta.title: Your team deserves
cta.subtitle: Free forever. No credit card required. Your first retrospective takes less than a minute to set up.
cta.notice: Works with any Scrum or Kanban team · Sign in with Google

featuresPage.title: Features — RetroDash
featuresPage.metaDesc: Explore all RetroDash features: real-time boards, private rooms, anonymous mode, gamification, AI writing, and more.
featuresPage.heroSubtitle: Private rooms, real-time boards, anonymous mode, gamification, AI writing assistance — all in one purpose-built tool.
featuresPage.realtimeDesc: Every card, vote, and status update propagates instantly to all participants via Firestore live listeners. No page refresh required — just an always-current shared board.
featuresPage.realtimePoint1: Cards appear the moment they're published
featuresPage.realtimePoint2: Votes update in real time for all participants
featuresPage.realtimePoint3: Facilitator controls broadcast to the whole room
featuresPage.privacyDesc: Private rooms require a password. Public rooms allow open access. Either way, your conversations stay inside your room.
featuresPage.privacyPoint2: Anonymous mode hides all author identities
featuresPage.actionsDesc: Action items have three states: Pending, Done, and Keep Going. At the end of a sprint, unfinished items can be imported directly into your next room — nothing gets lost.
featuresPage.actionsPoint2: Carry-over from any past retro into a new room
featuresPage.actionsPoint3: Linkable to the original feedback card
featuresPage.gamificationTitle: End every retro with a celebration.
featuresPage.gamificationDesc: The summary page shows a scoreboard with gold, silver, and bronze badges for top contributors. Points are awarded for published cards and action items created.
featuresPage.aiDesc: An 'Improve with AI' button on every card rewrites the text using Google Gemini. Regular cards become clear and constructive. Action items become specific, measurable, and verb-led.
featuresPage.aiPoint2: Separate modes for cards and action items
featuresPage.aiPoint3: 500 character limit to keep feedback focused
featuresPage.themingDesc: Light, dark, and system themes switch instantly without a page reload. The full interface is localized in English and Brazilian Portuguese.
featuresPage.themingPoint1: Light, dark, and system theme options
featuresPage.themingPoint3: Full localization in English and Portuguese (Brazil)
featuresPage.ctaSubtitle: Free to use. No credit card. Your first room is ready in under a minute.

howItWorksPage.title: How It Works — RetroDash
howItWorksPage.metaDesc: Learn how RetroDash works — from creating a room to walking away with clear action items in three simple phases.
howItWorksPage.heroSubtitle: Three phases, zero learning curve. Here is exactly what happens from creating a room to walking away with clear action items.
howItWorksPage.step1Desc: Log in with Google, click New Room, and configure your session: name it, pick your columns, and set it to private with a password or leave it public for open access.
howItWorksPage.step2Desc: If this is not your first sprint, pick any past ended room and choose which unfinished action items should land automatically in this new session.
howItWorksPage.step3Desc: Copy the room link or code from the confirmation screen and send it to your team via Slack, email, or any channel you use.
howItWorksPage.step4Desc: The facilitator clicks Start Retro to open the board for contributions. All participants can now add and vote on cards.
howItWorksPage.step5Desc: Each participant writes cards privately first in draft mode, then publishes them to the shared board when ready. No social pressure during writing time.
howItWorksPage.step6Desc: Everyone votes on any card except their own. Votes are toggleable — click again to remove. The most-voted cards surface to the top naturally.
howItWorksPage.step7Desc: Link action items to specific cards or add them directly to the Action Items column. Use the AI assistant to turn vague ideas into clear, verb-led tasks.
howItWorksPage.step8Desc: The facilitator ends the retro with an optional closing phrase. All participants are redirected to the Summary page automatically — no one misses the wrap-up.
howItWorksPage.step9Desc: The Summary page shows all participants, the scoreboard (in non-anonymous rooms), all action items ordered by votes, and a full recap of every card organized by column.
howItWorksPage.step10Title: Carry forward into the next sprint
howItWorksPage.step10Desc: Any unfinished action items from this session will appear as options the next time you create a room. Close the loop, sprint after sprint.
howItWorksPage.ctaTitle: Ready to run your first retro?
howItWorksPage.ctaSubtitle: Free to use. No credit card. Your first room is ready in under a minute.

useCasesPage.title: Use Cases — RetroDash
useCasesPage.metaDesc: See how Scrum teams, remote teams, and product teams use RetroDash to run better retrospectives.
useCasesPage.heroSubtitle: RetroDash adapts to your team's process — whether you run weekly sprints, async reviews, or anything in between.
useCasesPage.scrumDesc: Scrum teams need a retro tool that respects the ceremony without adding friction. RetroDash fits naturally into the sprint cadence: create a room at the end of each sprint, run the retro in 30–60 minutes, and walk away with clear action items tracked across sessions.
useCasesPage.scrumPoint1: Custom columns for any retro format (4Ls, Mad Sad Glad, Start Stop Continue…)
useCasesPage.scrumPoint2: Dot voting to surface the topics that matter most
useCasesPage.scrumPoint3: Action items carry forward to the next sprint automatically
useCasesPage.scrumPoint4: Scoreboard to recognize and celebrate team engagement
useCasesPage.remoteDesc: Remote teams often struggle with social dynamics in retros — some voices dominate, others go quiet. Anonymous mode levels the playing field. Everyone writes without pressure, votes without bias, and the result is feedback that actually reflects the team.
useCasesPage.remotePoint1: Anonymous mode hides all names and photos — even on the scoreboard
useCasesPage.remotePoint2: Draft cards let each person write privately before publishing
useCasesPage.remotePoint3: Real-time sync keeps everyone on the same board, regardless of location
useCasesPage.remotePoint4: Share via link — participants join without creating an account
useCasesPage.productDesc: Product teams use retros to extract learnings from launches, incidents, and planning cycles. Link action items directly to the cards that generated them, and track which actions get completed sprint over sprint with the carry-over system.
useCasesPage.productPoint1: Action items linked directly to the originating feedback card
useCasesPage.productPoint2: 3-state tracking: Pending, Done, and Keep Going
useCasesPage.productPoint3: Full retro recap organized by column and ordered by votes
useCasesPage.productPoint4: Carry-over surfaces unresolved actions in the next session
useCasesPage.ctaTitle: Your team's next retro starts here.
useCasesPage.ctaSubtitle: Free to use. No credit card. Your first room is ready in under a minute.
